message:
perf(btree): skip CellRef::parse for interior child_page_at

Every root-to-leaf descent probes the interior page's child pointers via
child_page_at. The old body routed through parse_cell_at → CellRef::parse,
which decoded the payload_size varint, the rowid varint, computed the
local payload size, and validated overflow bounds — all thrown away to
extract the 4-byte left-child pointer that sits at the very start of the
interior cell. It also polluted the CellSlotCache with interior-cell
entries that competed against genuinely useful leaf-cell slots.

child_page_at now delegates to the existing read_interior_child_inline,
which reads the cell pointer u16 and the 4-byte child page directly from
the page image. Same error semantics for the `>= cell_count → right_child`
branch and for zero-pointer corruption.

Before: 28.038 ns / call (parse_cell_at + CellSlotCache, warm cache)
After:   5.186 ns / call (direct u16 pointer read + u32 child read)
Delta:   5.41x speedup; ~70 ns saved per 3-level descent with interior
         probes; plus frees the per-cursor cell-slot cache for leaf probes
Bench:   cargo test -p fsqlite-btree --release --lib
         bench_child_page_at_interior_table -- --ignored --nocapture
         (children=128, iters=5_000_000)
Parity:  363/364 btree lib tests pass; the one failure
         prop_index_btree_structural_invariants_hold_after_random_mutations
         is pre-existing on clean HEAD and unrelated.
Bead:    bd-yafor

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
